blast_radius.summary: Medium-High. 14 tasks across three subplans touching one composition root (app.py, 3x weight), one shared response schema (execution/schemas.py, 2x weight), one central chrome component (InspectorPanel.tsx, 2x weight), plus 6 new backend files and 8 new frontend files.

phase_2a.weighted_score: Modified files: app.py (3x critical composition root) + execution/schemas.py (2x shared contract) + execution/routes.py (1x) + config.py (0.5x) = 6.5 weighted. New files are additions only. Risk: Medium-High due to composition-root and shared-schema touches.

phase_2b.weighted_score: 3 modified files, all low-criticality type/export files. Risk: Low.

phase_2c.weighted_score: InspectorPanel.tsx is the central composer chrome (2x weight). The deletions from it (handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback) are the primary risk. Risk: High.

blast_radius.overall_recommendation: Acceptable to proceed as a single umbrella PR with the blocking issues resolved. File count is moderate; the elevated risk comes from the specific files touched, not the volume.

one_way_doors.mitigation: Same-commit move. Both files import from the new location. Call sites unchanged. Fully reversible by reverting the commit. No ownership-semantics drift risk within Phase 2 because the function body is identical — the plan states 'The implementation is identical to the _verify_session_ownership function currently in execution/routes.py.' The risk of semantic drift is real in future phases but is not created by Phase 2.

one_way_doors.change: ValidationError gains error_code: str | None = None; ValidationCheck gains affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = ()
one_way_doors.mitigation: These are additive optional fields under Pydantic v2 strict mode. They are NOT backwards-compatibility shims in the No-Legacy sense — the No-Legacy policy prohibits running old-and-new branches simultaneously or preserving dead code paths. Adding optional fields to a frozen dataclass-equivalent Pydantic model with defaults is correct incremental design. No objection here.
one_way_doors.note: However, see blocking_issues: the producers of these fields are NOT updated in Phase 2A.

one_way_doors.change: Phase 2C deletes handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback from InspectorPanel.tsx
one_way_doors.mitigation: INSUFFICIENT. See blocking_issues. The plan states Phase 3A Task 8 relocates these side effects to subscriptions.ts, but Phase 2 merges before Phase 3A. Between Phase 2C merge and Phase 3A Task 8 implementation, validation-failure chat feedback is silently broken.

one_way_doors.change: Standalone Validate button removed from InspectorPanel.tsx in Phase 2C
one_way_doors.mitigation: Button is removed within the same umbrella PR; revertible by reverting the commit. The Validation row in the audit-readiness panel subsumes the button's function. Keyboard shortcut status is checked during the smoke (Task 9 Step 4). Acceptable as long as the auto-validate subscription is in place.

blocking_issues.title: Phase 2C deletes handleValidate side effects before Phase 3A Task 8 creates the replacement subscription
blocking_issues.description: Phase 2C Task 8 deletes handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, and sendValidationFeedback from InspectorPanel.tsx. The plan explicitly acknowledges that 'those side effects belong to the auto-validate subscription in subscriptions.ts — Phase 3A Task 8 already does this.' However, Phase 2 merges as an umbrella PR before Phase 3A. Phase 3A Task 8 (in 15a2-phase-3a-removals-part-2.md) is where these side effects are actually relocated into subscriptions.ts. Confirmed by reading 15a2: Task 8 Step 2 states 'Move them into the auto-validate subscription' with explicit code and tests. Between the Phase 2 umbrella PR merging and Phase 3A Task 8 landing, the validation-failure system-message injection and sendValidationFeedback LLM feedback are silently broken. Users get no chat notification when their pipeline fails validation. The plan's risk table in 14c acknowledges this dependency but does not provide a mitigation — it states 'Phase 3A must land before validation-chat feedback is lost' without ensuring they land together.
evidence: 14c Task 8 description: 'Also delete handleValidate and its callers (injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback) from InspectorPanel.tsx — those side effects belong to the auto-validate subscription in subscriptions.ts (Phase 3A Task 8 already does this).'

15a2 Task 8: 'But wait — handleValidate did real side effects: injecting a system message and sendValidationFeedback(result). Those side effects are valuable. Move them into the auto-validate subscription so they fire on auto-validate too.'
Umbrella plan §Sequencing: Phase 2A merges before Phase 2C; no mention of Phase 3A being in the same PR.
blocking_issues.required_resolution: One of: (a) Move Phase 2C Task 8's side-effect deletion into Phase 3A Task 8 — i.e., Phase 2C only removes the button UI, not handleValidate and its side-effect callers. Phase 3A Task 8 then simultaneously deletes and relocates. (b) Add Phase 3A Task 4 (subscriptions.ts auto-validate effect) and Task 8 (handleValidate relocation) to the Phase 2 umbrella PR — making the side-effect relocation atomic with the deletion. Option (a) is lower risk and cleaner. Option (b) expands the Phase 2 blast radius into Phase 3A territory.

blocking_issues.title: ValidationCheck.affected_nodes producers not updated in Phase 2A — provenance row component_ids always empty
blocking_issues.description: Phase 2A adds affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = () to ValidationCheck. The provenance row's _build_provenance_row() in service.py keys off check.affected_nodes to populate component_ids. However, the actual producer of identity_node_advisory ValidationCheck instances — validation.py line 1243 — does NOT pass affected_nodes. This was confirmed by reading the live code: ValidationCheck(name=_CHECK_IDENTITY_NODE_ADVISORY, passed=True, detail=...) at line 1243 sets no affected_nodes field. Since the default is (), the provenance row will always have empty component_ids. The jump-to-component feature for provenance warnings is silently inert for Phase 2's entire lifetime until Phase 3A or later updates validation.py. The test in test_service.py for test_provenance_warning_on_identity_advisory passes a manually constructed ValidationCheck with affected_nodes=('pass',) — this masks the production bug because it never exercises the real validation.py path.
evidence: validation.py:1243-1254: ValidationCheck(name=_CHECK_IDENTITY_NODE_ADVISORY, passed=True, detail=...) — no affected_nodes argument.
14a service.py _build_provenance_row(): 'component_ids = tuple(node_id for c in advisories for node_id in c.affected_nodes)' — silently empty when affected_nodes defaults to ().
14a test_service.py test_provenance_warning_on_identity_advisory: manually sets affected_nodes=('pass',); does not use the real validation pipeline.
blocking_issues.required_resolution: Phase 2A Task 3 (or as a new Task 2.5) must update validation.py's identity_node_advisory construction to pass affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,). This is a same-commit requirement per CLAUDE.md No-Legacy policy: adding a structured field without wiring its producers is shipping half-landed schema work. Also requires adding an integration test (not just unit test) that calls the real validation pipeline and asserts affected_nodes is populated.

warnings.title: Stale-fetch race condition in loadSnapshot — slow version-N response can overwrite version-N+1
warnings.description: The version-keyed cache short-circuit in loadSnapshot prevents redundant fetches when the version hasn't changed, but does NOT prevent a slow version-N fetch from overwriting a faster version-N+1 response. Scenario: user makes rapid composition changes. Version-1 fetch starts (slow). Version-2 fetch starts (fast). Version-2 response arrives, stored. Version-1 response arrives, overwrites version-2 snapshot. Panel transiently shows stale data. The response itself carries composition_version, so a monotonic write guard ('discard if response.composition_version < current cached version') would prevent the overwrite. This risk is not in the umbrella's risk table despite being a direct consequence of the auto-fetch-on-version-change architecture.
warnings.required_resolution: Add a monotonic-version write guard to loadSnapshot: after receiving the server response, check whether the cached snapshot's composition_version is already >= the response's composition_version before storing. If so, discard. This is a one-line addition to the set() call in loadSnapshot.

warnings.title: EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS/SINKS deletion not committed to when Phase 7 lands
warnings.description: The umbrella plan states that when Phase 7 introduces data_trust_tier: ClassVar, trust.py 'can be replaced with a one-line getattr call without changing the wire shape.' Under CLAUDE.md's No-Legacy policy, this replacement must DELETE EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS, EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_SINKS, PluginTrust, and classify_plugin() in the same Phase 7 commit. The plan does not commit to this deletion — it only notes the replacement is possible. Two sources of truth (allowlist + ClassVar) must not coexist. Flag for Phase 7 planning.
warnings.required_resolution: Add an explicit note in trust.py's docstring (not a TODO comment, which is ephemeral): 'When Phase 7 adds data_trust_tier: ClassVar, delete this module entirely and replace classify_plugin() callers with a direct attribute lookup. Do not maintain both.' Also file a filigree dependency from Phase 7A backend to this deletion.

warnings.title: getattr(cls, 'determinism', None) in test_trust.py — CLAUDE.md ban on defensive getattr
warnings.description: test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted uses getattr(cls, 'determinism', None). CLAUDE.md unconditionally bans hasattr and getattr used defensively. The codebase confirms that determinism IS defined on the base class (infrastructure/base.py:146) — all plugins inherit it. The None default is never reached. This is defensive programming against a case that cannot occur within the system-owned plugin architecture. Replace with cls.determinism (direct access) — if any plugin somehow lacks the attribute, the resulting AttributeError is the correct crash.
warnings.required_resolution: Replace getattr(cls, 'determinism', None) is Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL with cls.determinism is Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL in test_trust.py. The test becomes a direct attribute access, consistent with CLAUDE.md.

warnings.title: secret_service attribute name mismatch — plan uses app.state.secret_service but app.py uses app.state.scoped_secret_resolver for the injected type
warnings.description: Phase 2A Task 5 Step 4 wires ReadinessService with 'secret_service=app.state.secret_service'. However, grep of app.py shows: app.state.secret_service = WebSecretService(...) (the raw, unscoped service) and app.state.scoped_secret_resolver = ScopedSecretResolver(...). The existing ExecutionService, BlobService, and ComposerService all inject app.state.scoped_secret_resolver, not app.state.secret_service. ReadinessService._build_secrets_row calls list_refs with a user_id, which needs scoped resolution. If the plan wires the wrong attribute, secret resolution will either use the wrong scope or crash at runtime. The plan notes 'adapt to actual attr name' but leaves it to the implementer to discover.
warnings.required_resolution: The plan should explicitly specify app.state.scoped_secret_resolver (not app.state.secret_service) in the wiring step, matching the pattern used by all other services. The ReadinessService._SecretServiceLike Protocol must be verified against ScopedSecretResolver's list_refs signature.

recommendations.title: Consolidate auth helpers before a fourth API module is added
recommendations.description: Three API modules now carry duplicate getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse (api/client.ts internals, api/preferences.ts, api/auditReadiness.ts). The plan correctly defers consolidation to Phase 8 and documents the technical debt in a JSDoc comment. This is acceptable for Phase 2, but should be treated as a Phase 8 blocker (not 'when convenient'). The pattern should not appear in a fourth module before Phase 8 ships.
recommendations.action: Ensure the Phase 8 filigree issue for this consolidation is linked to future API modules as a blocker. If Phase 5b, Phase 6, or Phase 7 add frontend API modules, they should reference the Phase 8 consolidation issue.

recommendations.title: Sequencing: Phase 2B Task 1 (types) can land before Phase 2A merges — the plan underexploits this
recommendations.description: The umbrella notes that 14b Task 1 (frontend types + API client) can be developed in parallel because the wire shape is fully specified in 14a. However, 14b §Sequencing then says 'After Phase 2A is merged' as the recommended first step. This unnecessarily serializes the work. The TypeScript types and API client wrappers have zero runtime dependency on Phase 2A; they can be authored, tested, and committed on a parallel branch immediately after reading 14a §Task 1.
recommendations.action: If the umbrella PR uses a single branch, this is moot. If subplans land on separate branches and the sequence matters for CI time, clarify that 14b Tasks 1-2 can be committed immediately after 14a Task 1 is committed (not after 14a is merged).

recommendations.title: Tracer-bullet assessment: approach is justified, no rework needed
recommendations.description: The plan has 14 tasks across three subplans before a full end-to-end smoke. However, the A/B/C split IS the tracer pattern at coarser granularity — each subplan merges and is demonstrable before the next starts. Phase 2A can be demoed via curl against the backend before any frontend exists. Phase 2B can be demoed via vitest. Phase 2C closes the loop with a real staging smoke. This is appropriate for the domain complexity. No further splitting is recommended.

recommendations.title: Placeholder-to-real handoff between 14b and 14c: window of deliberate scaffolding is acceptable
recommendations.description: 14b ships placeholder ReadinessRowDetail and ExplainDialog. These are never mounted (InspectorPanel.tsx is untouched by 14b), so they are dead code from a user-visible standpoint. The 'DO NOT extend the placeholder' comment is load-bearing — it prevents scope creep into these files before 14c lands. The risk of confusion is low because no user-visible regression exists and the files are explicitly marked as scaffolding. Acceptable as designed.

recommendations.title: Component mounting: AuditReadinessPanel is correctly mount-agnostic
recommendations.description: The component uses only semantic/role attributes (aria-label='Audit readiness', role=section) and CSS class names with no inherited parent context. The plan correctly identifies that Phase 3's relocation only changes the mount point, not the component. No sticky/overflow assumptions were found in the rendered JSX. Approved.

pattern_alignment.service_architecture: ALIGNED — ReadinessService follows the service-then-routes pattern established by Phase 1A (preferences, execution). Constructor injection, Protocol-based dependencies, no Service Locator.
pattern_alignment.package_structure: ALIGNED — audit_readiness/ mirrors the existing catalog/, execution/, sessions/ package layout at L3.
pattern_alignment.layer_model: ALIGNED — audit_readiness is explicitly L3; it imports only from L0 (contracts), L1 (core), and peer L3 packages (composer/state.py, execution/schemas.py, sessions/). The tier-model enforcer should accept this without allowlist additions.
pattern_alignment.tier_model: ALIGNED — panel reads only Tier-1 data (session, validation result, catalog, secrets, settings); no new trust boundaries introduced.
pattern_alignment.tdd_shape: ALIGNED — all tasks follow failing-test-first pattern with explicit run commands.
pattern_alignment.frontend_stores: ALIGNED — useAuditReadinessStore follows the getInitialState + reset() pattern used by executionStore. Version-keyed cache is a sound design choice.

cross_plan_integrity.wire_contract_14a_to_14b: SOUND — ReadinessRowId and ReadinessStatus literals are mirrored exactly. TypeScript exhaustive 'never' arm fails the build on divergence.
cross_plan_integrity.placeholder_handoff_14b_to_14c: SOUND — placeholders are never mounted (InspectorPanel.tsx untouched by 14b); 14c replaces them in-place. Clean boundary.
cross_plan_integrity.phase_3_mount_point_migration: SOUND — 15b2 Task 9 explicitly owns the AuditReadinessPanel relocation from InspectorPanel.tsx to SideRail's siderail-slot-audit-readiness before InspectorPanel is deleted. Dependency is cross-linked in 14c §Sequencing.
cross_plan_integrity.handleValidate_to_subscriptions_ts: BROKEN — see B1. Phase 2C deletes the side effects before Phase 3A creates their replacement.

summary: Two blocking issues require resolution before implementation begins. B1: Phase 2C removes handleValidate and its side effects (injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback) from InspectorPanel.tsx while trusting Phase 3A Task 8 to add replacements in subscriptions.ts — but Phase 2 merges before Phase 3A, creating a guaranteed regression window where validation-failure chat feedback is silently dark. Resolution: defer the side-effect deletion to Phase 3A Task 8, or pull Phase 3A Task 4+8 into the Phase 2 umbrella PR. B2: Phase 2A adds affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = () to ValidationCheck but does NOT update validation.py's identity_node_advisory construction site to populate the field. The provenance row's jump-to-component feature is silently inert in production despite passing unit tests (which supply the field manually). Resolution: update validation.py in Phase 2A Task 3 or a new Task 2.5. Four warnings round out the review: a stale-fetch race in loadSnapshot (monotonic write guard needed), the Phase 7 allowlist-deletion commitment, a CLAUDE.md-prohibited getattr in test_trust.py, and a wrong attribute name for the secret service injection in app.py.
